bot0:arena:kv:policy.reasoning: Dying repeatedly to bows at range — lower kiteRangeBias to -4 to force aggressive closure before bow shots lock. Keep targetThreatAversion at 120 to prioritize escaping bow/spear threats over low-HP targets. Retain retreatFireWhileKiting to deter chasers during disengagement — proven effective in re

lessons: Grapple has 0 wins in last 9 rounds and 1 win in 19 total plays — abandon it entirely unless under pickup_surge (which has no data support).
Sword users (Warden, Executioner, Shredder, Reaper) caused 8 of last 10 deaths — engage only with sword or spear from cover, never with grapple.
Shield users (Juggernaut, Fortress) killed us 5 of last 6 rounds — grapple cannot penetrate shields; switch to spear or sword for direct damage.
Bow users (Deadeye, Andrew-Test, GhostProtocol) have 50+ kills vs us historically — avoid open ground; only engage from tight corridors or elevated positions.
Teleport_surge rounds saw 90%+ enemy use of spear and daggers — avoid this modifier unless armed with spear or sword.
Shredder and Reaper have killed us 3+ times each in last 5 rounds — both use sword/staff and exploit grapple’s range; retreat or flank aggressively on sight.
bot0:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Sword is the only weapon that has successfully killed multiple high-threat opponents (Warden, Reaper, Shredder) in recent rounds and counters the dominant enemy loadout.

bot2:arena:kv:policy.reasoning: Staff is dying repeatedly to grapple users (5/6 recent deaths). Lowering grappleCloseMinGap to 0.3 forces faster closes to prevent kiting, and setting kiteRangeBias to -4 maximizes pressure to close before grapple/bow users engage. Reducing targetThreatAversion from 80 to 70 avoids over-prioritizing

lessons: Grapple is highly effective against sword users under pickup_surge (9 kills in round 150, 6 in round 154) — prioritize grapple only when sword prevalence exceeds 60% in enemy weapon data.
Bow users (Deadeye, Ranger, Valkyrie) killed us 7 times in last 10 rounds — retreat immediately upon sighting them, especially in open areas or hazard_storm maps where mobility is crippled.
Viper (daggers) killed us in rounds 151 and 152 — never engage directly; grapple only if you can pull them into walls, corners, or environmental hazards.
Spear users (Valkyrie, Lancer) killed us 4 times in last 5 rounds — avoid direct confrontations; use shield users as cover or wait for hazard_storm to break line-of-sight.
Shield users appeared in 5 of last 10 rounds and never died to us — avoid initiating fights; let others engage them or use hazard_storm/environmental damage to weaken them.
Hazard_storm reduces our survival rate to 20% (4/5 losses) — grapple is lethal in this modifier; switch to sword or adopt defensive posture when it activates.
bot1:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Grapple fails under hazard_storm and against bow/daggers users, while sword has proven lethal against dominant sword-wielding enemies and offers better survivability in close quarters.

lessons: Grapple users (Hook, Scorpion) killed you in 5 of last 6 rounds — retreat from open areas and avoid mid-range engagements; their mobility outmaneuvers staff.
NeuralReaper (staff) killed you in round 151 despite lower Elo — avoid direct staff duels; they deal higher burst damage in close quarters.
Sword users account for 85% of enemy weapons seen and 7 of your 10 kills — prioritize eliminating them early before they close distance, especially in clean maps.
Shield users (Fortress, HexMind) caused 4 deaths — lure them into hazard_storm zones to force shield cooldowns, then burst with staff AoE before they re-engage.
Your only win (round 148) came from 3 quick sword kills under 2200 ticks — be aggressive in first 15 seconds against low-HP sword users in open maps.
hazard_storm reduces your survival time to 1600 ticks avg — avoid prolonged exposure; use storm to corner sword users, not to survive.
bot2:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Grapple outperforms staff in mobility and range against dominant sword and shield users, and has higher win rate in recent high-traffic encounters.
